authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 112 - Standards for the Growing, Harvesting, Packing, and Holding of Produce for Human Consumption
authoritative_sources.source_class: Primary regulators, statutes, or agency manuals
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR rule text is the primary federal authority for covered produce farm practices, records, and controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: field, water, soil amendment, harvest, packing, and holding control review
used_for: field and harvest food-safety evidence expectations
water and biological soil amendment control handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Anchors farm-level produce-safety evidence review and prevents unsupported release or compliance claims.

authoritative_sources.title: FSMA Final Rule on Produce Safety
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the administering federal regulator for the Produce Safety Rule and publishes official scope and workflow summaries for agricultural water, soil amendments, animals, equipment, and training.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: produce-safety applicability and operational workflow framing
used_for: seasonal water, animal, and equipment-control vocabulary
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Converts broad regulatory text into practical workflow stages while keeping final applicability decisions tenant- and authority-dependent.

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart S - Additional Traceability Records for Certain Foods
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The current eCFR text is the primary federal source for traceability lot codes, critical tracking events, key data elements, and record retrieval for covered foods.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: traceability lot, shipping, receiving, and recall-readiness handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines how traceability gaps become hold or escalation triggers.

authoritative_sources.title: Food Traceability List
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA maintains the official list used to determine which foods are subject to enhanced traceability requirements.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces the pack to request commodity and transformation context before applying enhanced traceability claims.

authoritative_sources.title: USDA Harmonized GAP and Harmonized GAP Plus+ Audits
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture Agricultural Marketing Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USDA AMS administers official GAP/GHP audit services and publishes public audit-scope expectations for farm review, field harvest, packinghouse, storage, and traceability evidence.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: farm audit evidence and buyer-program support
used_for: field harvest and packinghouse record expectations
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Shapes realistic artifact names and output packages while reserving certification decisions for authorized auditors or program owners.

authoritative_sources.title: 7 CFR 205.203 - Soil Fertility and Crop Nutrient Management Practice Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This USDA National Organic Program regulation governs soil fertility, crop nutrient, manure, and compost handling in organic operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: soil amendment, land, and program-claim dependency handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents unsupported organic or soil-amendment clearance when application timing and treatment records are missing.

authoritative_sources.title: Agricultural Worker Protection Standard
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA publishes official pesticide worker-protection requirements and guidance for agricultural settings.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: pesticide-adjacent field condition and safety-boundary handling
used_for: refusal of pesticide-label or agronomic signoff
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps pesticide-related requests routed to qualified owners instead of being handled as generic food-safety clearance.

authoritative_sources.title: Animal Disease Traceability
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Agriculture Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service
authoritative_sources.source_class: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APHIS is the federal animal-health authority and publishes official animal identification and movement traceability program materials.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: livestock movement and animal-traceability dependency handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Supports livestock-related food-safety escalation without implying veterinary or final disposition authority.

authoritative_sources.title: Seafood HACCP
authoritative_sources.source_class: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's Seafood HACCP materials are official public guidance for fish and fishery-product food-safety controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: fishery and aquaculture handoff evidence handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Covers the fishing portion of the industry overlay while keeping this lane focused on evidence packaging and escalation.

authoritative_sources.title: General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized standards bodies or public operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Codex is a globally recognized food-hygiene and HACCP framework used by regulators and food businesses for vocabulary and control-system concepts.
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Normalizes food-safety output language without substituting for controlling law or tenant procedure.

workflow_stages.name: intake and applicability gating
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, commodity, site, production-cycle phase, season, field or livestock context, Food Traceability List status where relevant, and tenant authority before making a food-safety recommendation.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, or agency manuals
Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: field, water, input, and harvest evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Review field logs, agricultural-water records, soil-amendment records, weather or runoff observations, animal-intrusion findings, harvest logs, equipment sanitation records, and worker hygiene evidence.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, nonconformance, and corrective-action handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible evidence supports continued hold, release recommendation, investigation, nonconformance record, corrective-action escalation, or incident escalation while preserving final authority boundaries.
source_classes: Primary regulators, statutes, or agency manuals
Recognized standards bodies or public operating references

workflow_stages.name: traceability and systems-of-record reconciliation
workflow_stages.description: Assess traceability lot codes, harvest lots, shipping and receiving KDEs, cooler or packing records, livestock movement records, and cross-system lineage before supporting any release posture.
source_classes: Production-cycle, traceability, or food-safety workflow references

workflow_stages.name: seasonal, environmental, animal, and program escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when weather, water, runoff, animal intrusion, livestock treatment, environmental conditions, organic or buyer-program rules, or regulatory interpretation materially change the safe answer.
source_classes: Seasonal, land, or livestock operating standards

artifacts: seasonal food-safety plans and harvest schedules
field logs, scouting logs, animal-intrusion logs, flood/runoff notes, adjacent land use context, and corrective-action history
agricultural water source inventories, inspection logs, water testing records, treatment records, and corrective-action records
biological soil amendment, compost, manure, nutrient, and application interval records
worker training, hygiene, equipment sanitation, harvest, cooling, packing, holding, pest-control, and audit records
traceability lot codes, harvest lots, cooler or pallet tags, shipping and receiving KDEs, inventory and transfer records
livestock movement, treatment, withdrawal, mortality, feed, health-event, and official identification records when food-safety posture is implicated
environmental reports, grant or program files, buyer pre-ship files, hold records, nonconformance reports, and incident logs
systems_of_record: field, land, GIS, and water-management systems
packinghouse, cooling, warehouse, ERP, WMS, and traceability systems
livestock, herd, flock, pond, or fishery management systems
quality management, nonconformance, CAPA, and incident systems
grant, program, audit, buyer-compliance, and document-control repositories

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites
assess whether visible agriculture food-safety evidence supports release recommendation, continued hold, investigation, nonconformance, corrective-action escalation, or incident escalation
prepare safety status summaries, hold or nonconformance records, corrective-action packages, incident summaries, and high-risk escalation notes with evidence references
route ownership to field operations, environmental compliance, food-safety, program administration, agronomy, animal production, supply-chain, legal, or formal QA owners
escalate: final release, harvest clearance, recall command, or regulator-facing communication
agronomic, pesticide-label, veterinary, environmental-permit, organic-certification, grant-program, financial-approval, or legal interpretation
field conditions, regulatory interpretation, buyer standards, tenant policy, or jurisdictional rules that materially change the answer
conflicting source, system-of-record, hold, traceability, water, livestock, or program evidence
refuse: override documented hold, release, harvest, traceability, water, sanitation, soil-amendment, livestock-withdrawal, or safety controls
treat incomplete or contradictory field, livestock, land, environmental, or traceability records as adequate proof
approve release, close incidents, certify compliance, or provide licensed, legal, environmental, veterinary, agronomic, or regulator-facing authority

domain_failure_modes: safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory but the requester pressures for release
a documented hold, unresolved issue, water deviation, animal intrusion, or traceability conflict is bypassed
repeat sanitation, agricultural-water, or input-control defects are treated as closed without corrective-action evidence
seasonal weather, flood, runoff, adjacent livestock, or environmental conditions change required workflow evidence but are ignored
field, livestock, land, cooler, shipping, or receiving records do not reconcile to the same harvest or traceability lot
the lane implies agronomic, veterinary, environmental, legal, grant, organic, recall, or final QA signoff
public sources, buyer requirements, tenant procedures, or jurisdictional rules conflict and are normalized into a confident answer

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Seasonal, land, or traceability dependency gap
Request that implies agronomic, veterinary, or environmental signoff

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Exact state, commodity-board, buyer, or grant-program rules
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack requires tenant-supplied jurisdiction, commodity, buyer, grant, organic, and program terms before applying case-specific conclusions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Produce Safety Rule and Food Traceability Rule applicability by commodity, transformation, exemption, and business size
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack uses FDA sources as the public baseline and blocks confident applicability or release claims until commodity, transformation, exemption, and tenant records are supplied.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Where food-safety support ends and agronomic, veterinary, environmental, or legal authority begins
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The manifest and scenarios explicitly refuse or escalate agronomic, veterinary, environmental, legal, grant, recall, and final QA authority requests.

notes: Research output constrains the final spec pack to evidence-backed farm-level food-safety support and explicit escalation for authority-sensitive decisions.

Source set covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling across produce safety, traceability, GAP/GHP audit evidence, organic input/land requirements, pesticide boundaries, livestock traceability, fisheries edge cases, and Codex hygiene vocabulary.
